Scholarships (study right started on or after 1 August 2025)
Aalto University has a scholarship programme available to tuition-fee students. Please note that the number of available scholarships is limited. There are also external scholarship possibilities (outside Aalto) to fund your studies.
Scholarship information on this page is for Aalto students who have started their studies on or after 1 August 2025.
Table of contents
Loading table of contents
Please note that the information on this page is for students studying at Aalto University, whose study right has started on or after 1 August 2025.
Information on scholarships for Aalto students whose study right has started on or before 1 August 2024 here
Information on scholarships and tuition fees for applicants here
Scholarship types
The Aalto University Scholarship Programme seeks to recognize talented non-EU/non-EEA students across the world by awarding academic excellence. If you are liable to pay tuition fees at Aalto, you can apply for the scholarships. Please note that the number of available scholarships is limited.
Aalto University does not offer any university-wide scholarships to cover living costs in Finland. Students are expected to cover all their living expenses and other study-related costs from their own financial resources. More information about the living costs is available from the
moving to Finland and financial matters page
.
International joint programmes that have their own tuition fees and scholarship systems follow their specific regulations – more about
scholarships in international double degree programmes
.
There are also
other scholarship possibilities
to finance your studies, including funding outside Aalto University.
Aalto University reserves all rights to make changes to the scholarship programme.
Scholarships and tax
The university is obliged by law to report all scholarships to Finnish tax authority on annual basis.
Scholarships in Finland are generally tax free up to the value of €26,269.46 per calendar year (in 2024; the exact amount changes annually). The amount exceeding the set limit is generally subject to tax. The set limit includes scholarships paid in Finland by a non-public entity including, but not limited to, any combination of Aalto University's scholarships (including tuition fee waivers), travel grants, one-off payments and living-expense scholarships you may get from the university for your studies.
If your combined scholarships and grants exceed the value of €26,269.46 for a calendar year (in 2024), the exceeding amount is generally subject to tax and you may be contacted by the Finnish tax authority.
For further information, please see the Finnish Tax Administration's pages on
taxation of students and trainees
and
taxation of grants and scholarships
.
Aalto University Excellence Scholarship
Aalto University Excellence Scholarships are awarded as tuition fee waivers, covering the full tuition fee. Tuition-fee students may apply for the scholarship during the admission process.
Aalto University Excellence Scholarships are merit-based and they are granted on a competitive basis to the highest-achieving applicants based on the programme's evaluation criteria. There is a very limited number of scholarships available, and programmes may also decide not to grant any scholarships.
Scope of the scholarship
Aalto University Excellence Scholarships cover the full tuition fee during the target time of the programme.
In order for the scholarship to be valid, you must agree to the terms of the scholarship by the given deadline.
Please note that Aalto University Excellence Scholarships do not cover living costs. Students need to cover all living expenses and other study related costs from their own financial resources.
Duration of the scholarship
The scholarships is granted for the target time of the degree programme. The target time is three years full-time study for bachelor’s degree and two years full-time study for master’s degree.
For the scholarship to continue to the second (or third in bachelor's) academic year, your study progress is checked annually. This is to confirm that you are making systematic progress in your studies according to your personal study plan.
The review of studies is done by the end of March in the spring term. If everything is all right, the scholarship is continued to the second (or third in bachelor's) academic year.
If there are any issues, such as missing credits or possible discontinuation of scholarship, they must be resolved by the end of April. This is to ensure there is enough time for enrolment (annual enrolment begins in May), and for students needing to renew their residence permits to get their documentation in order as early as possible.
The Aalto University Excellence Scholarship requires full-time study and studying according to the approved personal study plan.
Discontinuation of the scholarship
The progress of your studies is checked each term and your scholarship may be discontinued if your studies do not follow your personal study plan. If you have any issues affecting your study progress, it is essential that you contact the Student Services of your degree programme and actively seek help so that your scholarship is not affected. A failure to resolve any pending issues may result in the discontinuation of your scholarship on the basis of insufficient study progress.
As a guideline, scholarships are automatically continued for the standard duration of the degree programme unless:
studies do not follow personal study plan so that graduation is likely to be delayed by more than one term,
studies progress so slowly that graduation is likely to be delayed by more than one term,
studies progress below 45 ECTS credits per academic year leading to discontinuation of residence permit, or
the terms and conditions of scholarship agreement are broken.
In such cases the school will contact you and request a meeting where the scholarship is discussed and further course of action agreed.
Please note also, that the Finnish Immigration Authority (Migri) requires students to complete at least 45 ECTS credits per each academic year for the renewal of their residence permit. A failure to comply may result in the discontinuation of your residence permit and subsequent inability to complete your studies and continue your scholarship. Hence it is essential that you contact the Student Services as early as possible should you feel the need to revise your study plan for any reason.
The university is committed to helping students to achieve steady progress throughout their studies and offers many forms of assistance towards this aim.
Scholarships for Aalto bachelor's programme graduates continuing in Aalto master's programmes
If you have received the Aalto University Excellence Scholarship for your English-medium bachelor’s programme, you have a chance to obtain the Aalto University Excellence Scholarship also when continuing to the Aalto master's programme covered by your original 3+2 right to study.
Please note that the scholarship is available only for Aalto master’s programmes to which you have a direct right to study, i.e. without applying through an external master's admissions procedure. If unsure, please contact the student services of your degree programme for further advice.
Eligibility and scope of the scholarship
If you are a tuition-fee student who graduates from an Aalto University bachelor’s programme taught in English, you may receive an Aalto University Excellence Scholarship for your master’s studies if you have also received the Aalto University Excellence Scholarship for your bachelor’s programme.
In addition, you must complete the bachelor’s degree within the target time period (i.e. three years or six terms) with a weighted average grade of at least 3.50 to be eligible for the scholarship for the master's programme. The weighted average grade is calculated from the completed degree in its entirety.
The scholarship is granted as a 100% tuition fee waiver for the target time frame of a master’s degree (i.e. two years or four terms). The target time is considered to start directly after graduating as bachelor, except when registering as non-attending due to reasons specified in the law.
Scholarship applications and decisions
The scholarship is applied for in Sisu when filling in the bachelor’s degree graduation request. You will receive instructions for agreeing to the scholarship terms in connection with the scholarship decision. In order for the scholarship to be valid, you must agree to the terms by the given deadline.
If you haven't applied for the scholarship in Sisu when filling in the bachelor’s degree graduation request, but are eligible for the scholarship, please contact the student services of your degree programme.
Duration of the scholarship
The scholarship is granted for the target time of the master's programme (i.e. two years or four terms). The scholarship is to be used for the completion of degree studies only.
The scholarship is continued for the second academic year provided that you have progressed in your studies according to your approved personal study plan during the first academic year in your master's. In addition, you must have a goal-oriented and feasible study plan for the second year. The study progress is checked during spring term.
Discontinuation of the scholarship
The progress of your studies is checked each term and your scholarship may be discontinued if your studies do not follow your personal study plan. If you have any issues affecting your study progress, it is essential that you contact the Student Services of your degree programme and actively seek help so that your scholarship is not affected. A failure to resolve any pending issues may result in the discontinuation of your scholarship on the basis of insufficient study progress.
As a guideline, scholarships are automatically continued for the standard duration of the degree programme unless:
studies do not follow personal study plan so that graduation is likely to be delayed by more than one term,
studies progress so slowly that graduation is likely to be delayed by more than one term,
studies progress below 45 ECTS credits per academic year leading to discontinuation of residence permit, or
the terms and conditions of scholarship agreement are broken.
In such cases the school will contact you and request a meeting where the scholarship is discussed and further course of action agreed.
Please note also, that the Finnish Immigration Authority (Migri) requires students to complete at least 45 ECTS credits per each academic year for the renewal of their residence permit. A failure to comply may result in the discontinuation of your residence permit and subsequent inability to complete your studies and continue your scholarship. Hence it is essential that you contact the Student Services as early as possible should you feel the need to revise your study plan for any reason.
The university is committed to helping students to achieve steady progress throughout their studies and offers many forms of assistance towards this aim.
Scholarship for one additional term following the standard period of study (5th or 7th term scholarship)
In order to successfully complete your studies and apply for graduation, you may be awarded a scholarship for one additional term beyond the standard duration of the degree programme (i.e. one term in addition to the three years of a Bachelor’s programme, and one term in addition to the two years of a Master’s programme).
The scholarship for one additional term is available to all tuition-fee students, whose study right started on or before 1 August 2025.
Eligibility and how to apply
You can apply for the scholarship on the condition that your studies have progressed in accordance with the approved study plan and that you have a realistic, approved plan to complete the remainder of the programme.
You may be awarded the scholarship if the following criteria are met:
you have a goal-oriented and feasible plan for completing your studies,
your thesis topic has been agreed with the thesis supervisor during the last academic term covered by target period for completing the degree (applies to master’s degree students),
your thesis supervisor confirms that the thesis is progressing according to the plan.
You can normally apply for the scholarship between April and May, but applications may be considered at other times if the circumstances demand it.
Please note that the additional term is likely to impact on your residence permit, student accommodation and financial arrangements, so it is highly recommendable that you start planning as early as possible and use the help of student services in the process.
Scope of the scholarship and requirements
The scholarship for one additional term is offered as a 100% tuition fee waiver.
The scholarship has a strict requirement that you
submit your Bachelor's or Master's thesis (as applicable) by the agreed deadline,
successfully complete the programme, and
apply for graduation within the additional term.
Please note that the scholarship can be used only for completing the remaining compulsory studies in your programme. This means that you cannot apply for the additional term scholarship to e.g. complete studies outside your degree or go on exchange, unless an exchange period is a compulsory part of your studies.
If your programme includes a compulsory exchange period and you apply for the scholarship for one additional term to go on one, bear in mind that the scholarship terms require you to complete your studies and apply for graduation within the additional term. For example, course registrations after an exchange period might take some time, so it is good to take this into account when planning your studies.
Discontinuation of tuition fees
The collection of tuition fees may be discontinued for one academic term, immediately following the last term in the target period of the degree. The tuition fee discontinuation concerns the following terms: 7th term in bachelor's programmes or 5th term in master's programmes. You don't have to apply separately for the tuition fee discontinuation.
The tuition fee discontinuation is available to all tuition-fee students, whose study right starts on or after 1 August 2026.
Students in joint degree programmes are entitled to the discontinuation if they are within Aalto’s tuition-fee system after the target period of the degree.
Following the respective term, your tuition-fee payments will continue normally and you will be required to pay tuition fees for any terms for which you want to enrol as attending. If you are planning on graduating during the term, when the collection of tuition fees has been discontinued, please bear in mind that all graduation procedures have to be fully completed by the end of that term.
Note:
if you have received the Aalto University Excellence Scholarship for your bachelor's programme, please note that by extending your studies to the 7th term, you will not meet the scholarship criteria for obtaining the Aalto University Excellence Scholarship when continuing to the Aalto master's programme covered by your original 3+2 right to study.
Aalto Reserve Scholarships
Aalto Reserve Scholarships are aimed at students who become liable to pay tuition fees in the middle of their studies. The scholarship is offered as a 100 % tuition fee waiver.
You can apply for this scholarship if your residence permit status changes in the middle of your studies so that you are no longer exempted from tuition fees. You can also apply for a Reserve Scholarship if you are a non-EU/non-EEA student who continues their studies from a Finnish-medium bachelor's programme to an English-medium master's programme.
Please note that the Aalto Reserve Scholarships are not meant as reserve scholarships for those who did not receive a scholarship during admissions. In addition, the Aalto Reserve Scholarships cannot be used to waive the tuition fee while waiting for a residence permit decision.
Scholarship eligibility
In order to be eligible for the Aalto Reserve Scholarship, you need to prove that
you have been exempted from paying the tuition fees on the basis of your previous residence permit, but you will be liable to tuition fees based on your new permit type, OR
you have completed your bachelor's programme in Finnish and will become liable to tuition fees when continuing your studies in an English-medium master's programme within the same study right.
Please note that you must have a residence permit which does not exempt from tuition fees in order to be granted the Aalto Reserve Scholarship.
More information on exemptions from tuition fees
.
The scholarship decision can be made conditionally if
your residence permit application is still pending, OR
your average grade and accumulation of credits for the ongoing academic year cannot yet be determined. The credits relevant to the scholarship decision must be in the student register by the end of the ongoing academic year (31 July).
If your decision is conditional, you cannot register as attending before all conditionalities have been met.
Criteria for students whose residence permit status changes in the middle of studies
If your residence permit status changes so that you will become liable to pay tuition fees in the middle of your studies, you can apply for a Reserve Scholarship.
Students studying in English-medium bachelor's programmes
You may meet the eligibility conditions for the Reserve Scholarship if you meet the following criteria:
you would have received a tuition-fee waiver under the terms of the admissions process that existed when you applied to Aalto, and
you have a feasible study plan to complete your degree within the target period.
Students studying in master's programmes
If you have been admitted directly to the master's programme, you may meet the eligibility conditions for the Reserve Scholarship if you meet the following criteria:
you would have received a tuition-fee waiver under the terms of the admissions process that existed when you applied to Aalto, and
you have a feasible study plan to complete your degree within the target period.
If you have continued from a bachelor's programme within the same 3+2 study right at Aalto (3-year bachelor's, 2-year master's), you may meet the eligibility conditions for the Reserve Scholarship if you meet the following criteria:
you would have received a tuition-fee waiver during the bachelor’s admissions process,
you meet the scholarship criteria currently in force for transferring from an English bachelor’s programme to a master’s programme (grade average and graduation target time), and
you have a feasible study plan to complete your degree within the target period.
Criteria for students who continue from a Finnish-medium bachelor's to an English-medium master's
Bachelor's programmes completed in Finnish have no tuition fees. However, if you continue your studies within the same 3+2 study right to a master's programme taught in English, you will become liable to pay tuition fees for your master's studies.
You meet the eligibility conditions for the Reserve Scholarship if you meet the scholarship criteria currently in force for transferring from an English bachelor’s programme to a master’s programme (grade average and graduation target time).
Scholarship applications and decisions
You can apply for a Reserve Scholarship throughout the year, as there are no set application times. If you are eligible and meet the criteria, you are granted the scholarship. Only students currently studying at Aalto University may apply for the Reserve Scholarship.
If you want to apply for the Reserve Scholarship, please send a message to
tuitionfees@aalto.fi
to request the application form. Please note that it is compulsory to apply for the scholarship in order to receive it.
The scholarship application will be processed at your school and you will receive the decision to your email. You can accept the offered scholarship by signing the scholarship terms by the given deadline.
As defined in the Finnish Universities Act, the scholarship decision cannot be appealed to.
Duration of scholarship
The scholarship is granted as a 100 % tuition fee waiver for the target period remaining to complete the degree. The Reserve Scholarship will be valid earliest starting from the term
after the validity of your exemption based on your previous residence permit ends, OR
when you become liable to pay tuition fees.
If your scholarship decision has been conditional, the scholarship validity will start only after all the criteria and conditions have been met. Please note that you cannot register as attending if the decision is still conditional.
In case the scholarship is granted for more than one academic year, the study progress is checked annually to confirm that you are progressing in your studies according to plan.
For students in a bachelor’s programme, the scholarship is awarded only for completing the bachelor’s degree. Scholarships for master’s level studies (tuition-fee waivers) can be applied for when graduating with a bachelor’s degree.
Aalto University's scholarships in international double degree programmes
International joint programmes that have their own tuition fees and (externally funded) scholarship systems follow their specific regulations. In programmes where students are liable to pay the tuition fee directly to Aalto University, students are eligible to apply for Aalto University's scholarships. However, this only applies to the terms the student is studying at Aalto and is liable to pay tuition fee directly to Aalto University. Please read more from
programmes' web pages
.
The programme-specific tuition fee and scholarship policies are typically valid for the normative duration of the programme (two academic years). After this, the student may become liable for local tuition fees and eligible for related scholarships at Aalto University. The student should refer to the student or scholarship agreement they have signed with the programme.
Programmes with their own scholarship schemes
Advanced Materials for Innovation and Sustainability (EIT Raw Materials)
Cybersecurity and Assurance (CYBERSURE)
, starting from 1 August 2026
Environomical Pathways for Sustainable Energy Systems (SELECT)
Erasmus Mundus Joint Master Degree Bioceb
ICT Innovation (EIT Digital Master School)
Master’s programme in Smart Systems Integrated Solutions (SSIs)
Master's Programme in Communications and Data Science (CoDaS)
Master's Programme in Urban Mobility (EIT Urban Mobility)
Master's Programme in Manufacturing (EIT Manufacturing)
Re:Anima - European Joint Master in Animation
REPLAY - The European Game Design Masters (Erasmus Mundus)
Security and Cloud Computing (SECCLO)
Other scholarship possibilities
In addition to scholarships listed above, there are other scholarship opportunities to finance your studies. Aalto University offers some scholarships in collaboration with its partners, whereas others are fully offered by external scholarship providers.
Many of these scholarships are programme or country-specific. You can check out different possibilities and find the suitable scholarship for you.
Please note that if the scholarship does not cover the whole duration of your studies, you cannot apply for Aalto University's tuition fee waivers (Aalto University Scholarship / Aalto University Excellence Scholarship) in the middle of your studies.
Pekka Mattila Excellence Scholarship for applicants to Master's Programme in Marketing and Master's Programme in International Management
In memory of Pekka Mattila, these merit-based scholarships are aimed at particularly talented students admitted to the School of Business
Master’s Programme in Marketing
and
Master’s Programme in International Management
who are
liable to pay tuition fees
.
Scholarship amount
The Pekka Mattila Excellence Scholarships consist of a €3,000 relocation grant, paid at the beginning of the degree programme.
Who can apply for the scholarship?
Applicants who are required to pay tuition fees and are applying to the School of Business Master's Programme in Marketing or the Master's Programme in International Management are eligible to apply for the Pekka Mattila Excellence Scholarship, regardless of age, nationality, or gender.
Selection criteria
Selection for the Pekka Mattila Excellence Scholarship takes place during the admission process and is based on the academic evaluation of applicants according to the evaluation criteria outlined on the programme pages.
The Pekka Mattila Excellence Scholarship cannot be combined with the Aalto University Excellence Scholarship. Applicants may apply for both scholarships; however, if they are awarded the Aalto University Excellence Scholarship, they will no longer be eligible for the Pekka Mattila Excellence Scholarship.
How to apply for the scholarship?
Apply to the programme and Aalto University Excellence Scholarship
Submit your application to the School of Business Master's Programme in Marketing or the Master's Programme in International Management during the application period for Aalto University master's programmes. During this process, you can also apply for the Aalto University Excellence Scholarship with the same application form.
Submit the Pekka Mattila Excellence Scholarship form
After submitting your application to Aalto University, complete the Pekka Mattila Excellence Scholarship form stating your motivation and eligibility. The link to the application form is provided below.
Application form
Scholarship decision
Selected applicants will be notified in the same week that the Aalto University admission results are published.
Payment of the scholarship
The €3,000 grant will be transferred during the first year of the student's attendance at Aalto University. Payments will be made to Finnish or SEPA bank accounts for selected scholarship recipients after enrolment.
Contact information
If you have any questions regarding the Pekka Mattila Excellence Scholarship, please contact
tuitionfees@aalto.fi
.
COLFUTURO scholarship to master students from Colombia
COLFUTURO is a Colombian non-profit foundation that was established in 1991 with the support of the National Government and some of the most important companies of the private sector in the country at that time. Its main objective is to provide financial support and increase the possibilities of Colombian citizens to access high-quality postgraduate study programs abroad.
The financial support model they use is a Scholarship-Loan. COLFUTURO offers each student up to US$50,000, which may be requested in full, even if the program is one year. Students may be awarded scholarships for 80% of the loaned amount through incentive activities.
Aalto University has a collaboration with Colfuturo, which allows four Colfuturo granted applicants to additionally obtain an Aalto University partial discount equivalent to 25% on the tuition fee for the duration of the programme.
Available to
First year Master's programme applicants of all programmes at Aalto University with tuition fee liability at Aalto University (get in touch for eligibility in Joint programmes)
Citizens of Colombia who are required to pay tuition fees for Finnish university studies at Aalto University
Amount
A 25% reduction of the tuition fees for the normative period of study.
For more information on the terms and conditions:
COLFUTURO
.
Fulbright-Aalto University Graduate Award to master students from the United States
A prestigious scholarship for Aalto master’s degree students from the United States with the collaboration of Fulbright Finland Foundation. The award offers many outstanding benefits for the right candidate, including generous living allowance, comprehensive support services and excellent networking opportunities. Please note that the scholarship is available only to citizens of the United States.
You can find more information on the Fulbright Aalto University Graduate Award on the
Fulbright Finland Foundation's website
. If you have any further questions regarding the award at Aalto, please contact
tuitionfees@aalto.fi
.
The award has a dual application procedure where separate applications need to be submitted to the Fulbright grant competition and admission to the university. The application deadline for the award (from Fulbright) is typically in early October.
When applying to Aalto University, applicants can also apply for the Aalto University Excellence Scholarship in their admission application.
FUNED scholarship to master students from Mexico
FUNED MAESTRÍAS consists of a fund for maintenance and tuition fees paid directly to the candidate who will carry out master's studies abroad at one of the Top 200 universities. The funds for this call are made up of: 10% scholarship from FUNED + 90% credit from Eduk Pro. The total support of the scholarship plus the credit will be from 5,000 to 30,000 US dollars.
Aalto University has a collaboration with FUNED, which allows four FUNED scholarship recipients who have been accepted to Aalto University to additionally obtain an Aalto University partial discount equivalent to 25% on the tuition fee for the duration of the programme.
Available to
Citizens of Mexico who are required to pay tuition fees for Finnish university studies at Aalto University
1st year Aalto University Master's programme accepted students of all programmes with tuition fee liability at Aalto University (get in touch for eligibility in Joint programmes)
Amount
A 25% discount of the tuition fees for the normative period of study.
For more information on the terms and conditions:
FUNED (Fundación Mexicana para la Educación, la Tecnología y la Ciencia) for students from Mexico
.
Grants and awards
There are multiple grants and awards available to Aalto students. To learn more, please see the following page in the Student Guide, focusing especially on funding for research:
Grants and awards
.
Please note that Aalto University is not responsible for grants and awards coordinated by external parties. Before applying, please check carefully the criteria, schedules and other requirements related to the funding.
External funding
You can find several external scholarships and loan schemes suitable for international students liable to pay tuition fees at Aalto University on the
external scholarships page
.
You can also look for different scholarship opportunities from e.g. the
Scholarship Portal
.
Tuition fees and scholarships
